lnf_analysis.impact: Keyboard users can begin interacting with the sign-in form immediately.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus enters on the first main form field.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: Starting focus on the first sign-in field is a logical and meaningful entry point.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: It does not fail because landing on the email/username field is a logical entry point for a sign-in page and matches the primary task flow.

lnf_analysis.impact: Users can complete credentials in the natural sequence.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from the email/username field to the password field.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: Moving from username/email to password preserves the expected form completion order.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: It does not fail because this follows the expected left-to-right order of related form inputs in the sign-in process.

lnf_analysis.impact: Users can submit the form immediately after entering their password.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from the password field to the Sign In button.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: The transition from password to the primary submit button is logical for task completion.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: It does not fail because proceeding from entering credentials to the primary submit action is a reasonable next step.

lnf_analysis.impact: Users may momentarily lose their place or misinterpret the relationship and priority of the action buttons.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from the rightmost Sign In button backward to the leftward Cancel button.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: The focus order reverses the visible order of the two adjacent buttons, creating a backward jump.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: This reverses the visible left-to-right order within the same row, where Cancel appears before Sign In. A sighted keyboard user sees focus jump backward horizontally after reaching the rightmost button.

lnf_analysis.impact: Keyboard users encounter a non-linear form sequence and may miss the checkbox until after the primary actions.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from the Cancel button to the Remember Me checkbox located up and to the left.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: The checkbox is visually earlier in the form but is focused only after later controls, disrupting meaningful order.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: This transition jumps backward in reading order because the Remember Me checkbox is visually positioned before the action buttons and higher on the page, yet it receives focus only after both buttons.

lnf_analysis.impact: Users continue through the panel in a mostly understandable downward progression.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from the Remember Me checkbox to the Create Account link.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: Moving from the checkbox to the account-creation link follows a sensible downward sequence.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: It does not fail because this proceeds downward to the next visible interactive item in the lower portion of the panel.

lnf_analysis.impact: Users may believe they missed content or become disoriented because focus jumps back to the top after moving downward through the form.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from the Create Account link near the bottom of the panel to the Walt Disney World logo at the top of the page.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: Jumping from the lower panel back to the top logo creates a non-sequential and disorienting focus order.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: This is a major spatial jump upward that breaks reading order. After finishing lower panel content, focus unexpectedly returns to a header element that is visually earlier and above previously traversed items.

lnf_analysis.impact: Users must visually relocate focus repeatedly, increasing cognitive effort and reducing predictability.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from the top logo to the Forgot password? link inside the form.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: The transition from the top logo back into the form midstream continues the page's erratic focus sequence.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: After jumping to the header logo, focus re-enters the middle-right area of the form instead of continuing in a consistent top-to-bottom order; this indicates interactive elements are not arranged in DOM order consistent with the visual layout.

lnf_analysis.impact: Users may have difficulty understanding the relationship among field help, recovery, and the sign-in inputs because focus order does not match layout.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from the Forgot password? link on the right to the information icon by the email label on the left.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: The focus jumps backward from a right-side recovery link to a left-side earlier help icon, violating expected reading order.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: This moves backward across the same general form area, from a right-side item near the password label to a left-side item associated with the first field, which is visually earlier in reading order.

lnf_analysis.impact: Users can continue to the footer links after completing the main page content.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from the information icon to the first footer link.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: Moving from the last remaining form-area control to the footer is a reasonable downward progression.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: It does not fail because, after the remaining form-area element is reached, proceeding downward to the footer is broadly understandable, even though the earlier overall form sequence was irregular.

lnf_analysis.impact: Users can scan the footer predictably.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from Help & Guest Services to Site Map.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: The footer links follow a normal left-to-right sequence.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: It does not fail because the footer links proceed left to right in visible order.

lnf_analysis.impact: Keyboard navigation remains predictable in the footer.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from Site Map to Terms of Use.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: This footer transition matches the visible order.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: It does not fail because the sequence continues left to right across adjacent footer links.

lnf_analysis.impact: Users can continue navigating legal links without confusion.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from Terms of Use to Legal Notices.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: The footer continues in a consistent left-to-right order.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: It does not fail because the footer sequence remains visually aligned and linear.

lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from Legal Notices to Privacy Policy.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: The transition follows the visible footer order.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: It does not fail because this is the next adjacent footer link in reading order.

lnf_analysis.impact: Users can tab through the footer without unexpected jumps.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from Privacy Policy to Do Not Sell My Personal Information.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: This footer movement is linear and visually consistent.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: It does not fail because the footer links still progress horizontally from left to right.

lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from Do Not Sell My Personal Information to Children's Online Privacy Policy.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: The focus order remains aligned with the displayed footer link sequence.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: It does not fail because the next link appears immediately to the right in the footer.

lnf_analysis.impact: Users can reach the last footer item predictably.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus moves from Children's Online Privacy Policy to Your California Privacy Rights.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: The final footer transition continues the expected left-to-right order.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: It does not fail because this is the final adjacent footer link in visible order.

lnf_analysis.impact: Users complete navigation of the available interactive content.
lnf_analysis.issue: Focus exits the page after the last footer link.
lnf_analysis.one_sentence_reason: Exiting after the last footer link is expected and does not disrupt meaning.
lnf_analysis.why_it_fails: It does not fail because leaving the page's final visible interactive element is expected once all focusable items have been traversed.

navigation_path.description: First text input in the sign-in form, located in the left column under the Sign In heading.
Second text input in the sign-in form, positioned to the right of the email/username field.
Primary action button for submitting the sign-in form, located below the password field on the right.
Secondary action button located to the left of the Sign In button.
Checkbox option below the email field and to the left of the action buttons.
Link below the 'Don't have a Disney account?' text near the lower left of the sign-in panel.
Logo at the top center of the page, likely linking to the homepage.
Recovery link aligned to the right of the Password label above the password field.
Small informational/help icon adjacent to the email/username label.
navigation_path.element: Information icon near Email Address or Username label

navigation_path.description: First footer link at the bottom left area of the page.
Footer link to the right of Help & Guest Services.
Footer link to the right of Site Map.
Footer link to the right of Terms of Use.
Footer link to the right of Legal Notices.
Footer link to the right of Privacy Policy.
navigation_path.element: Do Not Sell My Personal Information link

navigation_path.description: Footer link to the right of Do Not Sell My Personal Information.
Last visible footer link at the bottom right of the page.
